title: Full README
text:
pristineR is an R package designed to simplify common data-cleaning and summarization tasks through safer, user-friendly helper functions.
The package focuses on preprocessing issues commonly encountered in real-world datasets, including: - inconsistent date formats, - messy column names, - and missing values.
This project was developed as part of my data science coursework and helped strengthen my experience with package development, documentation, reproducible workflows, and GitHub collaboration.

title: Full README
text:
pristineR is an R package designed to simplify common data-cleaning and summarization tasks through safer, user-friendly helper functions.
The package focuses on preprocessing issues commonly encountered in real-world datasets, including: - inconsistent date formats, - messy column names, - and missing values.
This project was developed as part of my data science coursework and helped strengthen my experience with package development, documentation, reproducible workflows, and GitHub collaboration.

title: Full README
text:
Motivation
Many datasets require extensive cleaning before analysis can begin. I created pristineR to streamline repetitive preprocessing tasks into lightweight helper functions that are easy to use and designed to fail safely when invalid inputs are encountered.
This project also gave me experience thinking about usability and documentation from the perspective of future users.

title: Full README
text:
Why pristineR?
Many real-world datasets contain inconsistent date formats, messy column names, and missing values that require repetitive cleaning before analysis can begin.
pristineR was created to simplify these common preprocessing tasks through lightweight helper functions that are easy to use, well documented, and designed to fail safely when given invalid inputs.

title: Full README
text:
Installation
You can install the development version of pristineR from GitHub with:
# install.packages("remotes")
# install.packages("devtools")
devtools::install_github("ADC-405-S26/pristineR")

title: Full README
text:
Load the Package
library(pristineR)

title: Full README
text:
Example Dataset
The package contains an example dataset called messy_survey.
The dataset intentionally contains inconsistent column names, mixed date formats, and missing values.

title: Full README
text:
Clean Column Names
Datasets imported from spreadsheets or surveys often contain spaces, punctuation, or inconsistent capitalization in column names.
The clean_names() function converts messy column names into snake_case format.
names(messy_survey)

cleaned_data <- clean_names(messy_survey)

names(cleaned_data)

title: Full README
text:
Parse Dates
Datasets frequently contain dates stored in multiple formats.
The safe_date_parse() function safely converts these values into standard Date objects.
cleaned_data$submission_date <-
  safe_date_parse(cleaned_data$submission_date)

cleaned_data

title: Full README
text:
Handling Invalid Dates
If a date cannot be parsed, safe_date_parse() returns NA and produces a warning message instead of stopping execution completely.
safe_date_parse(c("2024-01-01", "bad-date"))

title: Full README
text:
Generate Summary Statistics
The quick_summary() function generates common summary statistics for selected numeric columns.
quick_summary(
  cleaned_data,
  c("exam_score")
)
The summary includes: - mean, - standard deviation, - minimum, - maximum, - and missing value counts.

title: Full README
text:
Function Parameters

safe_date_parse(x)

x: a character vector containing date values



quick_summary(data, columns)

data: a data frame
columns: character vector of numeric columns to summarize



clean_names(data)

data: a data frame with column names to clean

title: Full README
text:
Additional Example
pristineR can also be used with other messy datasets.
example_data <- data.frame(
  "Customer Name" = c("Alice", "Bob"),
  "Purchase.Date" = c("2024-01-01", "01/15/2024"),
  "Total Sales $" = c(120, 95)
)

example_data <- clean_names(example_data)

example_data$purchase_date <-
  safe_date_parse(example_data$purchase_date)

example_data

title: Full README
text:
Package Functions
pristineR currently includes: - safe_date_parse() - quick_summary() - clean_names()

title: Full README
text:
Skills Developed
Through this project, I gained experience with: - R package development - Documentation writing - GitHub workflows - Reproducible analysis - Data cleaning pipelines - Error handling - User-centered function design

title: Full README
text:
Revisions After Feedback
Following peer review and instructor feedback, I expanded the project documentation to include: - clearer explanations of when and why to use each function, - additional edge case examples, - more detailed workflow demonstrations, - and improved descriptions throughout the README and vignette.
These revisions made the package easier to understand and more useful for future users.

title: Full README
text:
GitHub Repository
View the pristineR repository ````

title: Home
text:
Hi there! I’m Laura (pronounced “LOR-uh”). I am an Undergraduate Student at Lawrence University studying Biochemistry, Data Science, and Anthropology. I’m a Wisconsin native and have kept my roots here ever since I was born. Prior to my time at Lawrence, I graduated near the top of my class from my alma mater, Plymouth High School, where I first developed my love for science.
In the future, I plan to attend a Forensic Science graduate institute in order to specialize in Forensic Toxicology. My academic interests combine laboratory science, analytical chemistry, and computational analysis.
One of my favorite courses at Lawrence has been analytical chemistry, where I developed strong laboratory skills and gained experience with precise quantitative techniques and scientific methodology. I especially enjoyed spending long hours in the lab focusing on accuracy, troubleshooting, and reproducible scientific work.
Through my explorations of research at Lawrence, I came to discover that data processing was something I hadn’t previously considered, but quickly realized was an incredibly valuable skill to have. With this, I discovered my enjoyment of data science, creating informative figures, and using computational analysis to support technical lab reports and scientific communication.



Biochemistry
Analytical Chemistry
Forensic Toxicology
Scientific Research
Data Science
Data Visualization

In addition to my academics, I compete on the Lawrence Cross Country and Track and Field teams in my extra time. Running has continued to be one of my passions since I was young, and I’m very grateful to represent my team here.
In addition to running and my studies, I also enjoy singing, watching movies, playing videogames, and practicing my flute (when I have the time).
```

title: Research & Experience
text:
One of the most influential courses in my academic experience has been analytical chemistry. Through extensive laboratory work, I developed strong attention to detail, quantitative reasoning skills, and precise laboratory techniques. I especially enjoyed spending long hours in the lab focusing on accuracy, troubleshooting experiments, and reproducible scientific methodology.
This course strengthened my interest in scientific research and reinforced my appreciation for careful, data-driven laboratory work.



Quantitative laboratory analysis
Scientific documentation
Precision measurement techniques
Experimental troubleshooting
Reproducible scientific workflows

title: Research & Experience
text:

Through extensive laboratory work, I developed strong attention to detail, quantitative reasoning skills, and precise laboratory techniques. I especially enjoyed spending long hours in the lab focusing on accuracy, troubleshooting experiments, and reproducible scientific methodology.
This course strengthened my interest in scientific research and reinforced my appreciation for careful, data-driven laboratory work.



Quantitative laboratory analysis
Scientific documentation

title: Research & Experience
text:
Data Science Experience
Through my coursework and research experiences at Lawrence University, I discovered my interest in data science and computational analysis. What began as a useful skill for processing scientific data quickly became one of my favorite parts of research.
I particularly enjoy: - Creating informative visualizations - Cleaning and organizing datasets - Producing reproducible workflows - Combining computational tools with scientific research - Communicating technical results clearly

Technical Skills

R Programming
SQL
Quarto
GitHub
Data Visualization
Statistical Analysis
Text Analysis

title: Research & Experience
text:
Research & Scientific Communication
I have also worked extensively on scientific literature interpretation and presentation development related to molecular biology and biochemistry topics.
These experiences helped me develop: - Scientific communication skills - Figure interpretation abilities - Presentation design experience - Critical analysis of scientific literature - Collaboration and teamwork skills

title: Research & Experience
text:
Athletics & Leadership
Outside of academics, I compete on the Lawrence University Cross Country and Track & Field teams. Athletics has taught me discipline, time management, teamwork, and perseverance — skills that have also strengthened my academic and research work.
Balancing athletics with STEM coursework has helped me become highly organized and adaptable in fast-paced environments.

title: Research & Experience
text:
Future Goals
In the future, I hope to pursue graduate study in forensic science with a specialization in forensic toxicology. I am especially interested in combining analytical chemistry techniques with computational analysis to support scientific investigation and research. ```

title: Projects
text:
This page highlights projects involving data science, scientific communication, programming, and computational analysis.
Throughout these projects, I developed experience with: - R programming - Data visualization - Statistical analysis - SQL - Text analysis - Scientific communication - GitHub workflows - Reproducible research practices

title: Projects
text:
Featured Projects

pristineR Package
An R package designed to simplify common data-cleaning and summarization tasks through user-friendly helper functions.
Skills emphasized: - R package development - Documentation - Data cleaning pipelines - Error handling - GitHub collaboration



SQL Project
A project focused on querying and analyzing structured datasets using SQL.
Skills emphasized: - Database querying - Data organization - Relational databases - Data interpretation



Text Analysis Project
A computational text analysis project involving pattern recognition and visualization of textual datasets.
Skills emphasized: - Text mining - Natural language processing - Data visualization - Statistical analysis



Poster Project
A scientific communication project focused on presenting technical material clearly and effectively through visual design.
Skills emphasized: - Scientific communication - Figure design - Data presentation - Visual storytelling



Research Beyond Class
Additional scientific and research experiences outside of coursework, including literature interpretation and presentation development.
Skills emphasized: - Research analysis - Scientific literature review - Presentation design - Biochemistry communication ```
